reasoning: Ethiopia currently has a Level 3 travel advisory ("Reconsider Travel") from the US State Department, as explicitly confirmed by a reissue alert published mere days before the knowledge cutoff (April 25, 2025). This reissue explicitly stated its purpose was to remove obsolete COVID-19 page links, suggesting the broader security situation did not warrant overriding the Level 3 designation. While there is severe ongoing regional conflict—particularly between the ENDF and Fano militias in Amhara, as well as clashes in Oromia—these specific zones are already subject to regional "Level 4 – Do Not Travel" warnings within the countrywide Level 3 advisory. A transition to a countrywide Level 4 typically requires a precipitous, catastrophic breakdown in security directly threatening the capital or an impending general evacuation, neither of which is indicated right now. Given the extremely short forecast horizon of under two months (May 2 to July 1, 2025), the probability of the State Department upgrading the entire nation of Ethiopia to Level 4 is robustly low.
